# ===========================================================================
# Python Dependencies for the MLB Player Stats Backend
# ===========================================================================
# Install all dependencies with: pip install -r requirements.txt
#
# Each library serves a specific purpose in the stack:

# --- Web Framework ---
# FastAPI: Modern, high-performance Python web framework for building APIs.
# It provides automatic request validation (via Pydantic), auto-generated
# Swagger UI docs at /docs, async support, and type-based routing.
# This is the core framework that defines all our API endpoints.
fastapi

# Uvicorn: ASGI server that actually RUNS the FastAPI application.
# FastAPI defines the endpoints, but uvicorn is what listens for HTTP
# requests and passes them to FastAPI. The [standard] extra installs
# uvloop (faster event loop) and httptools (faster HTTP parsing).
# Run with: uvicorn main:app --reload
uvicorn[standard]

# --- Database ---
# databases: Async database interface that works with FastAPI's async/await.
# It manages a connection pool and supports non-blocking queries so the
# server can handle other requests while waiting for database responses.
# The [postgresql] extra installs the PostgreSQL-specific dependencies.
# Used for all runtime queries: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE via await database.execute()
databases[postgresql]

# asyncpg: The async PostgreSQL driver that 'databases' uses under the hood.
# It's the fastest Python PostgreSQL driver because it's written in C and
# uses PostgreSQL's binary protocol (not text). You never import this
# directly — the 'databases' library uses it automatically when the
# connection string includes "+asyncpg".
asyncpg

# SQLAlchemy: SQL toolkit for Python. We use SQLAlchemy Core (not the ORM)
# to define our table schema in models.py. It builds SQL queries as Python
# objects: players.select(), players.insert().values(...), etc.
# Also used for metadata.create_all() to create tables on startup.
# Note: We use SQLAlchemy for query building + schema, but 'databases'
# for actually executing queries asynchronously.
sqlalchemy

# psycopg2-binary: Synchronous PostgreSQL driver needed ONLY for
# metadata.create_all() at startup (which is a sync operation).
# The "binary" variant includes pre-compiled C extensions so you don't
# need PostgreSQL development headers installed on your system.
# All async queries use asyncpg instead — this is just for table creation.
psycopg2-binary

# --- Data Analysis ---
# Polars: High-performance DataFrame library (like Pandas but faster).
# Written in Rust, it's used here to compute:
# - League-wide averages (pl.col().mean())
# - Per-player derived stats (.with_columns() with column arithmetic)
# - Team-level aggregations (.group_by().agg())
# - Dynamic search filtering (.filter() with conditional expressions)
# Polars is the core data analysis engine of this project.
polars

# --- Configuration ---
# python-dotenv: Loads environment variables from a .env file into os.environ.
# This lets you store config like DATABASE_URL in a .env file instead of
# setting environment variables manually. Used in database.py to load
# the database connection string. The .env file is not committed to git
# (it often contains passwords and secrets).
python-dotenv

# --- MLB Data ---
# MLB-StatsAPI: Official Python wrapper for the MLB Stats API.
# Provides easy access to real-time and historical MLB statistics including:
# - Player batting/pitching stats by season
# - Team rosters and standings
# - Game schedules and scores
# - Player biographical info and positions
# Free to use, no API key required. Used by mlb_data_fetcher.py to
# fetch real player stats and populate/update the database.
# Documentation: https://pypi.org/project/MLB-StatsAPI/
MLB-StatsAPI

# --- Fantasy League Integration (ESPN + Yahoo) ---
# httpx: Modern async HTTP client for Python. Used to call the ESPN Fantasy
# API to fetch league scoring settings. Unlike the standard `requests` library,
# httpx supports async/await which is needed for FastAPI's async endpoints.
# We use it in espn_fantasy.py to fetch league data from:
# https://lm-api-reads.fantasy.espn.com/apis/v3/games/flb/...
httpx

# requests-oauthlib: OAuth 2.0 support for the Yahoo Fantasy Sports API.
# Yahoo Fantasy uses OAuth 2.0 with an authorization code flow.
# This package wraps the 'requests' library with OAuth session management,
# handling token exchange, refresh, and Bearer token injection.
# Used in yahoo_fantasy.py for:
#   1. Generating the Yahoo authorization URL (user opens in browser)
#   2. Exchanging the authorization code for access/refresh tokens
#   3. Auto-refreshing expired tokens and making authenticated API calls
# Also installs 'requests' and 'oauthlib' as dependencies automatically.
requests-oauthlib
